bridge_concepts: Tryptophan hydroxylase circadian regulation in dinoflagellates

checks.name: KEGG_pathway_crosscheck
checks.detail: Plant melatonin biosynthesis enzymes (EC 2.3.1.87, EC 2.1.1.4) verified in KEGG map00380. Symbiodiniaceae ABSENT from KEGG organism database (infrastructure gap, not negative evidence).
checks.warning: Dinoflagellate melatonin pathway is ANIMAL-type (TPH-first), NOT plant-type (TDC-first). Bridge operates through PRODUCT convergence.

checks.name: STRING_interaction
checks.detail: AANAT-ASMT interaction score 0.994 (very high). ARNTL/BMAL1 connection 0.671 confirms circadian regulation. IDO1/IDO2 0.931-0.933 links to stress-induced tryptophan catabolism.

checks.name: PubMed_cooccurrence
checks.detail: melatonin+coral bleaching: 0 papers. melatonin+Symbiodiniaceae: 0 papers. melatonin+dinoflagellate: 29 papers. KEY PAPER FOUND: Roopin 2013 (PMID 23496383) shows melatonin enhances photoprotection in Symbiodinium.
checks.warning: Roopin 2013 MUST be cited. Melatonin decreases photosynthesis rates but enhances photoprotective mechanisms in Symbiodinium.

checks.name: quantitative_plausibility_ROS
checks.detail: Direct OH scavenging covers ~24% at resting [melatonin] ~200nM. Cascade x10 multiplication projects full coverage. Enzyme induction (SOD, CAT, APX) effective at nM. Stressed dinoflagellates reach ~215 uM (Antolin 1997).
checks.warning: Generator MUST emphasize signaling/enzyme induction and cascade, not direct scavenging at nM concentrations.

checks.name: UV_photolysis
checks.detail: t_1/2 at reef depth 5-15m: 16-50+ hours. Nocturnal synthesis timing provides natural UV protection. Melatonin accumulates during darkness.

checks.name: GEO_expression_check
checks.detail: Datasets exist (PRJNA723630, SRP315798) with Symbiodiniaceae heat stress transcriptomes but TPH/AANAT genes not yet analyzed. Mining these is a key testable prediction.

key_paper_discovered.finding: Melatonin enhances photoprotective mechanisms in Symbiodinium; nocturnal peaks; decreases photosynthesis rates

overall_rationale: 4/5 checks passed (1 inconclusive). AANAT-ASMT verified at 0.994. Field disjunction confirmed (0 papers). Quantitative plausibility supported with cascade multiplication. Key mechanistic paper (Roopin 2013) validates the bridge.
warnings_for_generator: Dinoflagellate pathway is TPH-first (ANIMAL type), not TDC-first (PLANT type) — product convergence, not pathway homology
Direct scavenging at nM concentrations covers only ~24% of ROS. Emphasize signaling/enzyme induction and cascade mechanisms
MUST cite Roopin 2013 (PMID 23496383) — shows melatonin enhances photoprotection in Symbiodinium
Melatonin DECREASES photosynthesis rates (Roopin 2013) — reconcile with photoprotection
No KEGG data for Symbiodiniaceae — use primary literature for mechanism grounding
Propose mining PRJNA723630 (Camp 2022) for TPH/AANAT expression under heat stress as first test
